'''Paul John Loughlin''' (born 28 July 1966) is an English former [[rugby league]] {{rlp|ce}} who played for his hometown team [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], the [[Bradford Bulls]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Swinton Lions]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Loughlin was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], [[Lancashire]], England. He made his first team début for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] against [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] aged 17 in 1984.<ref>{{cite web|last1=de la Rivière|first1=Richard|title=Paul Loughlin|url=http://richarddelariviere.co.uk/?p=509|publisher=WordPress|access-date=29 July 2014|date=28 December 2010}}</ref> He went on to score over 2,000 points for the club, and holds the club record for most points scored in a game - 2 tries and 16 goals for a total of 40 points in a 112–0 victory over [[Carlisle RLFC|Carlisle]] during September 1986.<ref name="shs" /> In 1995, he was transferred to the [[Bradford Bulls]] as part of the record breaking deal to bring [[Paul Newlove]] to [[Knowsley Road]].

Loughlin was also a regular selection for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was selected to go on the [[1988 Great Britain Lions tour]].

Loughlin played right-{{rlp|ce}} scored 2-[[Try (rugby)|tries]] and kicked 3 goals, and was [[player of the match|man of the match]] in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 15-14 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[1987–88 League Cup (rugby league)|1987–88 John Player Special Trophy]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Saturday 9 January 1988.

After his move to Bradford Bulls, he enjoyed several successful seasons with the Bradford Bulls. He played for the Bradford Bulls at {{rlp|ce}} in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> Loughlin then played a prominent role in the Bradford Bulls winning the [[Super League]] title in 1997. After spending two further seasons in the Super League with the [[Huddersfield Giants]], he ended his career with the [[Swinton Lions]].

Loughlin appeared in a total of five [[Challenge Cup]] Finals, three times for St. Helens, and twice for the Bradford Bulls, and was a member of the losing side on each of these occasions.<ref>{{cite web|title=Cup heroes: Paul Loughlin|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/3407723.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=29 July 2014|date=27 February 2004}}</ref>

Loughlin is now head coach of amateur rugby league team Garswood Stags ARLFC who have gained promotion to Northwest Men's League Division 4.
